Lucas Dewey
Experience Contact
Back to experience

2017-2018

TrackR

R&D Mechanical Engineer

Concepted and prototyped Bluetooth tracking integrations for the Works with TrackR program, creating partner-facing product mockups and improving TrackR hardware accessories through injection molded part design, prototyping, and cross-functional collaboration.

Location Santa Barbara, CA

Highlights

  • Supported the Works with TrackR partner program by developing physical concepts for Bluetooth tracking integration across consumer product categories.
  • Created prototypes and product mockups for partner presentations, working across mechanical, electrical, firmware, and marketing teams.
  • Improved TrackR Pixel accessory usability through waterproof sleeve concepts and battery door texture updates, while mentoring an intern on injection molding DFM.

Tools

SolidWorksPrototypingProduct mockupsInjection moldingSilicone part designDFMConsumer electronics packagingBluetooth tracking integrationCross-functional collaboration

Projects

Work at TrackR

Project

Works with TrackR product concepts

Developed physical product concepts showing how Bluetooth tracking technology could be embedded into partner-company products.

Contribution

  • Mocked up multiple integration concepts including pens, multi-tools, keychains, flexible card formats, and other consumer products
  • Worked with electrical, firmware, and marketing teams to align mechanical concepts with technical feasibility and partner presentation needs
  • Built prototypes and presentation models to communicate product possibilities clearly

Result

Helped support partner discussions by turning Bluetooth tracking integration ideas into tangible product concepts.

Project

TrackR Pixel waterproof sleeve

Designed an injection molded waterproof silicone sleeve concept for the TrackR Pixel to improve durability and expand everyday-use protection.

Contribution

  • Developed a protective sleeve design around the existing TrackR Pixel form factor
  • Balanced waterproofing, fit, flexibility, and manufacturability for a molded silicone accessory
  • Applied DFM principles for injection molded part design

Result

Created a manufacturable accessory concept to improve product robustness and usability.

Project

Battery door usability improvement

Improved the TrackR battery replacement experience by designing a textured battery door pattern for easier removal and handling.

Contribution

  • Responded to customer feedback around battery door usability
  • Designed surface texture options to improve grip during battery replacement
  • Considered molded part constraints while refining the user-facing detail

Result

Improved the service interaction for a common user task with a small, targeted molded-part update.

© Lucas Dewey. Portfolio content is limited to public or non-confidential work. Confidential project details, implementation specifics, and proprietary visuals are intentionally omitted.
